programmes.show_description: Gnaws: The team face the horrifying prospect of confronting a giant man-eating creature living in the sewer system. Can they discover what it is and how it was created?
Super Secret Cypher Snatch: Steed and Tara investigate the deaths of secret agents attached to Cypher HQ.
Private Charles Lamb: When the 4077th is invited to share an Easter meal with a Greek unit, tender-hearted Radar comes up with a creative way to save the main course.
Bombed: Radar reaches HQ, who concede that they are being bombed, but not by the enemy. It's their own artillery. A patient comes into the OR with a grenade wired to his body.
Bulletin Board: Trapper writes to his daughter about life at MASH; smelly tent, smelly doctors. The bulletin board says Henry is going to give a lecture on sex.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Aloha: Mark and Jonathan travel to Hawaii to help a singer overcome her self-pity and pride. A friend of hers reveals that he has AIDS due to a blood transfusion he received years ago.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
A Dolphin Song For Lee: Part 1: A teenage cancer patient is dying, and has one more chance for a lifesaving operation. She has one dream that she would like to see fulfilled.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
The Correspondent: A war correspondent is captured and faces execution by revolutionaries. Jonathan and Mark show him what he's missed during his life by putting work ahead of family.
Aloha: Mark and Jonathan travel to Hawaii to help a singer overcome her self-pity and pride. A friend of hers reveals that he has AIDS due to a blood transfusion he received years ago.
The Love of Johnny Johnson: Friction grows between the Ingalls sisters when young Laura falls for handsome Johnny Johnson who, despite all her efforts, seems to only have eyes for Mary.
If I Should Wake Before I Die: Certain that only her funeral will bring her family to Walnut Grove for an overdue visit, Amy Hearn convinces Doc Baker and the Ingalls to fake her death.
Town Party, Country Party: Laura befriends Olga, a girl with one leg shorter than the other, who is teased by Nellie. But when Charles helps Olga, he finds resistance from the girl's father.
The Wedding: Part 1: Mary Ellen agrees to marry Dr David Spencer, and the couple waste no time planning their wedding. Meanwhile, Dr Curtis Willard arrives to be the town doctor.
The Wedding: Part 2: Mary Ellen announces her engagement to young David Spencer, and, after the initial shock, the Waltons join forces with the community to plan the big day.
Par for the Course: A roaming stranger wanders around Ballykissangel as Siobhan and Brendan argue over their daughter's christening.
The Last Hurrah: Part 1: Arthur's career and reputation hang in the balance when a patient's son makes an incriminating allegation against him.
A Simple Operation: Dr Finlay takes a walking holiday in the Highlands, but his expedition is rather more passionate than expected.
Rumpole and the Honourable Member: Rumpole reluctantly agrees to defend an MP who is accused of raping a party worker.
The Consultant: Hawkeye and Trapper meet Dr Borelli, a veteran doctor in Tokyo, whom they convince to visit the 4077th. Borelli intervenes when Frank is about to amputate a crushed leg.
House Arrest: Hawkeye is put under house arrest for punching Frank but is treated like a conquering hero. Meanwhile Hot Lips is nervous about a visit from Colonel Reese, the head of nurses.
Aid Station: Hawkeye, Margaret and Klinger are sent to a frontline aid station under heavy fire to fill in for a dead surgeon.
Fantasy action with Christopher Lambert and Sean Connery. After duelling through the ages, the last remaining immortals gather for an epic showdown in NYC - and there can be only one survivor. (1986)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Fantasy action with Christopher Lambert and Sean Connery. After duelling through the ages, the last remaining immortals gather for an epic showdown in NYC - and there can be only one survivor. (1986)
Dirtier By The Dozen: Steed, Purdey, and Gambit investigate a British Army division when a general disappears during an inspection of their base. Will camera footage hold the answers?
Game: The Avengers are forced to solve a jigsaw puzzle when its pieces appear in the hands of dead men. Steed and Tara find themselves at the mercy of the Games Master.
The Consultant: Hawkeye and Trapper meet Dr Borelli, a veteran doctor in Tokyo, whom they convince to visit the 4077th. Borelli intervenes when Frank is about to amputate a crushed leg.
House Arrest: Hawkeye is put under house arrest for punching Frank but is treated like a conquering hero. Meanwhile Hot Lips is nervous about a visit from Colonel Reese, the head of nurses.
A New Life: Henry Willows has been divorced for several years and is happy and content living alone. But his home life is quickly turned upside down when his son Matthew asks to stay.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
A Dolphin Song For Lee: Part 2: Lee undergoes an operation which doctors hope will cure her cancer. Mark helps her lobby in the state senate for broader environmental protection.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Heaven Nose, Mister Smith: Bob Hope guest stars as an angel dispatcher who assigns Jonathan to stop an angel intent on breaking up his son's marriage.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Teleshopping.
The Last Summer: Jason Carter starts working for Ruthy Leland. During their time together he learns the power of friendship as well as the painful lessons that sometimes come with it.
For The Love Of Blanche: Mr Edwards is left in care of a baby orangutan named Blanche, whom Mrs Olesen wants destroyed after it attacked Nancy at school.
May I Have This Dance?: When Willie announces his plans to marry sweetheart Rachel Brown and manage the hotel-restaurant bearing his sister's name, Mrs Oleson tries to interfere.
Hello And Goodbye: Mr Edwards faces a moral dilemma when Philip Rogers, the biological father of his recently adopted son Matthew, shows up to reclaim the boy.
...To Yesterday: Albert decides to stay in Walnut Grove after he receives some heartbreaking news. Meanwhile, the schoolchildren prepare for the traditional climbing of the mountain. (1983)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
...To Yesterday: Albert decides to stay in Walnut Grove after he receives some heartbreaking news. Meanwhile, the schoolchildren prepare for the traditional climbing of the mountain. (1983)
...Prairie: Pilot: Feature-length pilot based on the true story of the Ingalls family and their decision to move west in search of a better life. Stars Michael Landon and Melissa Gilbert. (1974)
A Harvest of Friends: After moving his family from Wisconsin to Minnesota, pioneer Charles Ingalls settles on farmland by Plum Creek.
Country Girls: Mean Nellie Oleson snubs Laura and Mary for wearing homespun dresses on their first day at school, and Caroline reminds Laura to be kind to Nellie to win her friendship.
The Hundred 100 Mile Walk: When their wheat crop is ravaged by a hailstorm, the Walnut Grove men leave town to search for work. Charles hears about a well-paid but dangerous job.
Mr Edwards' Homecoming: Charles meets up with Isaiah Edwards, a man the Ingalls befriended on their journey to Minnesota. Back at Walnut Grove Laura is ill in bed, which concerns Mr Edwards.
The Love of Johnny Johnson: Friction grows between the Ingalls sisters when young Laura falls for handsome Johnny Johnson who, despite all her efforts, seems to only have eyes for Mary.

...Of Darkness: Crime thriller with Jerome Flynn and Sadie Frost. Quiet accountant Martin is made partner at work, wins the lottery and falls in love. Is his luck about to come to a deadly end? (1999)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
